content.text:
Now I have a clear view of the molecule. Looking at atom C:0 (carbon with index 0), I can see its connections:

- Atom C:0 is connected to C:1 (carbon atom with index 1)
- Atom C:0 is connected to H:21 (hydrogen atom with index 21)
- Atom C:0 is connected to H:22 (hydrogen atom with index 22)

Let me confirm this by examining the structure more carefully. The atom labeled "C:0" is on the left side of the molecule, and it has three bonds: one to C:1, and two to hydrogen atoms (H:21 and H:22).

The geomconf_index for this molecule appears to be 1861, as indicated by the filename "geomconf_1861.xyz".

So the neighbor atoms of atom 0 (C) are:
- Atom index 1, element C
- Atom index 21, element H
- Atom index 22, element H

Geomconf_index: 1861

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 0, whose element should be C. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
